meta.display_name: Resolve conflicting DEI training accommodation requests for long-tenure employees

task:
There's some confusion arising from two conflicting emails we have received from Emma Thompson and Rachel Foster about the format of the DEI wrkshop for Design employees. We need to resolve.

But, before doing anything, reach out to Rachel Foster on chat to establish if her directive still stands and the exact justification she wants communicated to Emma. Also, talk to Sarah to get guidance on how to communicate this type of cross-departmental communication to Talent Acquistion Leadership. Some tips on tone and language would be helpful.

After you've done this, email Emma and rachel about the conflct. There may be some tension, so carefully point out that as Design Director, Rachel's directive takes precedence and then outline Rachel's reasoning behind her decision. This may help to defuse the tension.

Either way, Rachel's word is final and so set up a 30-minute DEI Training Format Alignment this week with both Emma and Rachel. 

Finally, DM Sarah Johnson via chat outlining the situation and resolution, making sure to use Sarah's preferred framing. Thanks!

agent_profile_overrides.secret: I absolutely stand by my directive — no exceptions. When you email Emma, I want the reasoning stated as follows: 'The Design Director has confirmed that live in-person attendance is mandatory for all Design team members because the interactive exercises in the DEI Foundations workshop are essential to building the cross-functional empathy our department's inclusion strategy depends on, and the asynchronous format does not replicate that experience.' Please use that exact reasoning.

I appreciate being kept in the loop. When you message me about this escalation, please frame it as: 'Flagging for Talent Acquisition leadership visibility: a format conflict between Design management levels on DEI Foundations training has been resolved in favor of the Design Director's mandatory in-person attendance policy. An alignment meeting has been scheduled between the parties.' That's the framing I want used — concise and neutral.

seed_calendar_events.description: Review sprint progress with design team

seed_emails.subject: DEI Workshop Accommodation Requests
seed_emails.body_text:
Hi HR,

I'd like to request that four of our long-tenure Design department employees — Oliver Chen, Lucas Anderson, Maya Patel, and Sophia Martinez — be switched from the in-person DEI Foundations workshop to the virtual asynchronous format. They're all deep into a critical project deliverable right now, and pulling them out for a full-day in-person session would really set us back. The virtual option would let them complete the training on their own schedule without impacting our timeline.

Please let me know if this can be arranged.

Thanks,
Emma Thompson
Design Manager

seed_emails.subject: Hold on DEI Training Changes
seed_emails.body_text:
Hi HR,

I wanted to flag something before any changes are made. I want all Design team members to attend the live in-person DEI Foundations session — no exceptions. The interactive component is critical for our department's inclusion goals, and the asynchronous version simply doesn't deliver the same impact. I'm aware some of my managers may request format switches due to workload, but I've made this a department priority and need everyone present.

Please do not approve any format change requests for Design without checking with me first.

Best,
Rachel Foster
Design Director
